title: Multi-language docstring extractor for JSDoc, Python, C#, and Doxygen formats
purpose: Parses source code to extract structured documentation from various docstring formats and associates them with method definitions.
module_context: Exports a function that maps method names to extracted docstring information, supporting multiple programming languages with specific extraction logic for each.

methods.name: extractDocstrings
methods.normalized_params: (language: 'ts' | 'js' | 'py' | 'cs' | 'cpp', source: string, methods: Array<{ name: string; start_line: number; end_line: number }>): Map<string, DocstringExtraction | DocstringAmbiguity>

steps.content: Split the source code into individual lines.
Initialize an empty map to store the extracted docstrings keyed by method name.
Select the appropriate extraction function based on the specified language.
For each method definition, invoke the selected extractor with the source lines and method info.
IF the extractor returns a result, check if the method name already exists in the map.
IF the method name already exists, store an ambiguity indicating multiple candidates.
ELSE store the extraction result under the method name in the map.
Return the populated map of method names to docstring extractions or ambiguities.

methods.name: ambiguity
methods.normalized_params: (kind: DocstringAmbiguity['kind'], message: string, start: number, end: number): DocstringAmbiguity

steps.content: Construct and return a new DocstringAmbiguity object with the provided kind, message, and line range.

methods.name: makeExtraction
methods.normalized_params: (title: string, purposeLines: string[], stepItems: string[], tags: { deprecated?: boolean; since?: string }): DocstringExtraction

steps.content: Construct and return a new DocstringExtraction object with the provided title, purpose, steps, and tags.

methods.name: parseTagLine
methods.normalized_params: (line: string): { tag: string; body: string } | null

steps.content: Match the line against a regular expression to extract a tag and its body.
IF the line matches the expected format, return an object containing the tag and body.
ELSE return null indicating no valid tag was found.
Locate the start and end of the JSDoc block above the method.
IF the docstring is too far from the method, return an unattached ambiguity.
Extract and clean the lines within the JSDoc block.
Parse the content of the docstring into title, purpose lines, and steps.
IF no explicit steps are found, attempt to extract them from purpose lines that are numbered or bullet-pointed.
Construct and return a docstring extraction with the parsed information.
Identify the start of the Python docstring following the method definition.
IF the docstring is too far from the method, return an unattached ambiguity.
Extract and dedent the lines of the docstring.
Parse the docstring content into title, purpose lines, and steps based on section headers.
IF deprecated or version tags are found, set the corresponding flags in the tags object.
Construct and return a docstring extraction with the parsed information.
Locate the start and end of the C# XML doc comment above the method.
IF the docstring is too far from the method, return an unattached ambiguity.
Extract and join the lines of the XML doc comment.
Parse the XML content to extract summary, remarks, and tag information.
IF deprecated or since tags are found, set the corresponding flags in the tags object.
Construct and return a docstring extraction with the parsed information.
Attempt to extract the docstring using the JSDoc extractor first.
IF the JSDoc extraction succeeds, return that result.
ELSE locate the start and end of the Doxygen comment above the method.
IF the docstring is too far from the method, return an unattached ambiguity.
Extract and clean the lines of the Doxygen comment.
Parse the content of the Doxygen comment into title, purpose lines, and steps.
Construct and return a docstring extraction with the parsed information.
